items.title: CBD vs THC
items.summary: CBD is non-intoxicating; THC is the primary intoxicating compound subject to regulation.

items.title: Industrial hemp
items.summary: Hemp policies focus on fiber and low-THC products under separate rules.

items.title: Netherlands coffee shop culture
items.summary: Tolerance policies are region-specific and distinct from full legalization.

items.title: Uruguay legalization model
items.summary: National framework emphasizes regulated access and public health safeguards.

items.title: Portugal decriminalization
items.summary: Public health focused reforms replace criminal penalties for possession.

items.title: Thailand policy shifts
items.summary: Policy changes are evolving and require verification for tourism guidance.

sources.title: Sources and references
sources.subtitle: Every record must link to official sources for verification and transparency.

faq.question: Is this platform advocating for cannabis?
faq.answer: No. This platform is educational and focuses on legislation, public policy, and regulatory context.

faq.question: Where does the data come from?
faq.answer: Each record links to official sources. Verify and update before public publication.

faq.question: How often is data updated?
faq.answer: Updates depend on your pipeline; each country record includes a last updated field.
